description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:248 (sign) and :299 (get_public_key) map every non-throttling, non-not-found service error to SecretError::Backend (permanent). AWS docs and the aws-sdk-kms model classify KmsInternalException, DependencyTimeoutException, and KeyUnavailableException as transient/retryable.

Trigger: a transient KMS server-side blip (e.g. KmsInternalException, frequently observed during regional events) returns Err(SecretError::Backend{..}) instead of Err(SecretError::Unavailable{..}).

Bad behavior: callers that implement the documented retry-with-backoff contract on Unavailable (per SecretError docs in secretx-core/src/lib.rs:999-1012) will NOT retry, and a transient KMS blip becomes a hard sign() failure visible to the application as a non-retryable error.

Sister crate secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:97-109 already classifies is_internal_service_error() as Unavailable; KMS should do the same plus DependencyTimeoutException and KeyUnavailableException (use the generated is_kms_internal_exception(), is_dependency_timeout_exception(), is_key_unavailable_exception() helpers in aws-sdk-kms 1.105).
title: AWS KMS retryable errors mapped to permanent SecretError::Backend
